summary: Isotropic Gaussian blur: a normalized sampled Gaussian (radius ceil(3*sigma), sigma->0 identity cutoff) convolved through the direct convolution reference.

inputs.name: input
inputs.doc: The Image or Field1 to blur (each channel blurred independently).

outputs.name: output
outputs.doc: The blurred Image or Field1 (same kind and extent as the input).

params.name: sigma
params.doc: The Gaussian standard deviation in pixels; sigma <= 1e-3 is the identity.

params.name: mode
params.doc: The boundary mode forwarded to the convolution.

params.name: sigma_max
params.doc: Upper bound on sigma; a larger request is rejected.

perceptual.reason: filter.gaussian_blur convolves a normalized sampled Gaussian verified by analytic properties (unit-sum positive kernel, constant preservation, 90-degree isotropy, the sigma-semigroup G_s1*G_s2 ~ G_sqrt(s1^2+s2^2), the blurred-impulse variance match, and the sigma->0 identity cutoff) and a differential check against filter.convolve with the same kernel; there is no perceptual-quality metric to apply
